title: Complete OSC 21 special-color storage
description: Finish Kitty OSC 21 beyond the representable first slice by adding terminal/front-end storage and rendering paths for cursor_text, selection_foreground, selection_background, visual_bell, and transparent background slots. Preserve OSC 21 query semantics, decide whether replies should be batched into one OSC 21 response, and add focused tests plus conformance evidence.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Reuse existing TermColors, NamedColor indices, ColorRequest PTY reply path, renderable_content selection rendering, and frontend background plumbing. Store OSC 21 special colors in small in-house terminal state rather than adding a theme/color framework. Rejected alternatives: depending on a CSS color crate (parsing is already in-house), moving all renderer colors into backend in one refactor, or implementing transparent-background compositor behavior before the existing background pipeline is understood. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed the OSC 21 special-color storage/query slice and wired the special colors into existing render paths: added keyed slots for cursor_text, selection_foreground, selection_background, visual_bell, and transparent_background; selection colors now affect grid selection rendering and Ghostty shader uniforms; cursor_text now affects block-cursor glyph inversion and Ghostty shader uniforms; queries/resets use real ColorRequest/TermColors slots. Decision: keep one OSC 21 reply per requested key, matching the existing dynamic-color reply path instead of batching. Split actual visual-bell overlay and transparent-background compositor behavior into yzt-deq because those require renderer/window policy beyond safe color storage. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend kitty_color tests; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm --features wgpu; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Fix X11 IME callback cast warning
close_reason: Completed. Replaced the direct function-item-to-integer cast in the X11 IME preedit start callback transmute with an explicit function-pointer-to-raw-pointer-to-integer cast, preserving the existing XIM ABI shim while removing the Rust warning. Validation: cargo fmt -- --check, cargo check -p rioterm --no-default-features --features wgpu,x11,wayland, cargo test -p rio-window.

title: Add OS-backed OSC 99 notification handles and activation callbacks
description: Extend Rio's notifier layer so OSC 99 notification IDs map to platform notification handles. Support OS-level update/replace and close where platforms expose it, and route activation/button/close callbacks back to the originating PTY when a=report or c=1 request reports. This is intentionally split from the backend protocol slice because rio-notifier currently accepts only title/body and discards OS notification IDs.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before Rust work: use existing rio-notifier dependencies only. Production crates: existing zbus on Linux, existing objc2-user-notifications on macOS, existing windows crate on Windows. Dev-only crates: none. New crates rejected: notify-rust/libnotify wrapper, because zbus is already present and direct D-Bus access is needed for notification IDs, CloseNotification, ActionInvoked, and NotificationClosed. In-house logic: OSC 99 routing metadata, notification ID/action formatting, small Linux handle registry. Packaging impact: no new dependencies.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 99 OS-backed notification routing. Backend now preserves a=report, c=1, p=buttons, route id, and close metadata; emits KittyNotification and CloseKittyNotification events; and Linux support queries advertise action/close/buttons. Frontend sends activation/button/close reports back to the originating PTY. rio-notifier now has NotificationRequest/Tracking/Event APIs, Linux D-Bus replaces_id tracking, CloseNotification support, ActionInvoked/NotificationClosed signal routing, and explicit untracked behavior for macOS/Windows until their delegate/event layers exist. Updated docs. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p rio-notifier, cargo test -p rio-backend ... kitty_notification, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, git diff --check, and a live Rio OSC 99 smoke.

title: Split fork-owned and validation-only terminal docs
close_reason: Split fork-owned additions from validation-only evidence: fork_feature_verification.md now contains only fork-owned runtime/tooling/policy/docs additions, and docs/yazelix/validated_not_added.md records inherited, baseline, benchmark, parser-only, source-audit, and deferred surfaces.

title: Fix focus-regain redraw burst from animation dirty rearming
description:
Problem:
Focusing yazelix-terminal can make the window briefly unresponsive while it rapidly renders lagged visual changes before input responsiveness returns. Local code inspection points at fork-added event-mode shader/trail redraw rearming rather than vanilla Rio.

Scope:
- Patch event-mode animation scheduling so shader/trail animation can request paced follow-up frames without marking terminal pending_update dirty and triggering immediate redraw loops.
- Keep terminal damage retries intact for real PTY/UI dirty state and terminal-lock deferrals.
- Avoid temporary logs unless they are permanent metrics or easy to disable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Animation-only redraws are paced through the scheduler instead of immediate dirty-after request_redraw loops.
- Terminal-damage redraw behavior is preserved.
- Focus regain with packaged shader/trail defaults no longer has a multi-second catch-up burst, or remaining lag is narrowed with evidence.
close_reason: Implemented event-mode animation pacing fix: shader/trail/progress redraws no longer mark terminal pending_update dirty, so animation-only frames use the existing vblank scheduler instead of immediate dirty-after redraws. Verified with cargo fmt --check, rioterm WGPU/X11/Wayland cargo check, Ghostty shader tests, and packaged event-mode smoke.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Post-fix comparison evidence, 2026-06-02:

User reports that Yazelix running inside vanilla/upstream Rio does not show the focus-regain lag or fast catch-up rendering bug, while Yazelix in yazelix-terminal has been less stable. This confirms the symptom is not inherent to Yazelix, Zellij, or the workload.

If the symptom persists after commit 219095be67 is installed, this bead's dirty-rearm fix should be treated as a likely mitigation or partial fix, not final proof of root cause. The next suspect remains fork-added animation/event scheduling rather than vanilla Rio behavior.

Updated docs/yazelix/fork_feature_verification.md to separate fork-owned additions from pre-existing Rio/terminal surfaces that are only validated, refreshed the ledger through commit 0880df924a, removed the stale X11 IME warning note, and added packaged cursor shader/event-mode smoke evidence. Validation: git diff --check.

title: Expose Yazelix Terminal flake app and checks
description: Promote the fork flake surface from inherited Rio outputs to Yazelix-owned outputs: packages.yazelix-terminal/default, apps.yazelix-terminal/default, a compatibility rio alias, and basic build/conformance checks for downstream Yazelix consumption.

Verified nix fmt -- flake.nix, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yazelix-terminal --no-link, nix run .#yazelix-terminal -- --version, nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.conformance --no-link, app program evals, and git diff --check.

title: Yazi Kitty graphics previews render corrupted in Yazelix Terminal
description: Yazi image and PDF previews inside Yazelix Terminal render as repeated/corrupted horizontal slices even though text input/resizing works. Treat as a terminal Kitty graphics rendering/placement bug first; validate with a minimal Kitty image probe and then with Yazi image/PDF previews in a Yazelix session.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal Kitty graphics probe renders correctly without sliced/repeated corruption. Yazi image preview and PDF preview render correctly in Yazelix Terminal. Regression coverage or durable validation evidence is recorded.
notes: Root cause: image overlay source_rect ABI diverged by renderer. WGPU and Kitty virtual/atlas code use [u0, v0, width, height], but native Vulkan GLSL and Metal shaders interpreted zw as [u1, v1], corrupting non-zero-origin Yazi preview slices into repeated horizontal bands. Fix: standardize Vulkan/Metal image shaders and GraphicOverlay docs on origin+size, add a sugarloaf regression test that all image-overlay shaders use the same ABI. Validation: cargo fmt --check, sugarloaf image_shaders_use_origin_size_source_rect, rio-backend kitty_virtual tests, conformance verify, nix build .#yazelix-terminal, direct Yazi image fixture screenshot, direct Yazi PDF fixture screenshot.
close_reason: Fixed Yazi image/PDF preview corruption by standardizing image-overlay source_rect semantics as [u0, v0, width, height] across Vulkan, Metal, WGPU, atlas graphics, and Kitty virtual placements. Validated shader ABI regression test, Kitty virtual tests, conformance harness, package rebuild, and direct Yazi image/PDF fixture screenshots.

title: Yazelix/Zellij interactions feel high-latency in Yazelix Terminal
description: When Yazelix Terminal launches yzx enter, Zellij interactions such as creating panes and pane navigation feel slow compared with the expected host-terminal behavior. Investigate whether latency comes from Rio event/render scheduling, packaged renderer strategy, Yazelix/Zellij integration, graphics wrapper, or terminal protocol handling. Acceptance: capture a reproducible benchmark/probe, identify the dominant cause, and either fix it or document the blocker with concrete source paths and next steps.
notes: Source investigation found that packaged game mode was being used as both an event-loop wake strategy and a full-damage policy. Renderer::run set force_full_damage = has_active_changed || is_game_mode_enabled, so every scheduled game-mode frame rebuilt every visible terminal row across Zellij panes. Changed the damage policy so game mode alone does not force full damage; active-panel changes still do. Validation so far: cargo fmt, cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p rioterm game_mode_alone_does_not_force_full_damage, cargo check -p rioterm, git diff --check, nix build .#yazelix-terminal-fast, Home Manager switch to local terminal commit 98d3419d29abaca02c8b63f9d603ed3c7346648b. Awaiting maintainer manual test of Zellij pane creation/navigation latency before closing and pushing.
close_reason: Superseded by yzt-ff9 after manual test showed the full-damage game-mode experiment made Zellij latency worse. Reverted the renderer experiment locally; proper fix is event-mode wake/redraw correctness rather than changing game-mode damage semantics.

title: Bring yazelix-terminal to Ghostty parity
description:
Make yazelix-terminal a Rio-derived terminal that can plausibly replace Ghostty for Yazelix: Ghostty-compatible cursor shader behavior, modern terminal protocol parity, Yazelix mode without terminal-native workspace ownership, and validated runtime behavior against Ghostty, WezTerm, Kitty specs, Zellij, Yazi, and Helix.

This is an experiment repo. Main Yazelix integration waits until local evidence proves the terminal is viable.
close_reason: Completed the Ghostty-parity experiment loop: all child beads are closed, current contract has no open Ghostty parity blockers, and remaining full-Kitty OSC 5522 arbitrary-MIME provider work is explicitly documented as frontier rather than Ghostty parity. Final gates: nix develop -c cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, git diff --check.

title: Record Rio fork lineage and experiment guardrails
description: Record upstream Rio base commit, remotes, license/attribution boundary, reference-source paths, branch policy, and the rule that main Yazelix integration waits for evidence. Acceptance: docs exist, repo builds or baseline build failure is recorded, and no feature work starts without lineage notes.
close_reason: Completed: recorded Rio upstream lineage, experiment remotes, license/source guardrails, reference clone commits, main Yazelix integration boundary, hard-problem rule, and baseline toolchain status.

title: Validate core protocols through Zellij Yazi and Helix
description: Validate Kitty graphics, Kitty keyboard, Sixel/iTerm2 images, OSC 8, OSC 52, bracketed paste, focus events, SGR mouse, synchronized output, and image-preview flows through the Yazelix stack.
notes:
Integration validation will avoid changing or pushing the main Yazelix repo. Use the built yazelix-terminal binary as host where possible, prefer current packaged/installed Yazelix runtime entrypoints, and separate automatic PTY/protocol fixture evidence from manual Zellij/Yazi/Helix screenshots. If a workflow requires human interaction, document exact reproduction steps and leave code untouched.

Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new production crates. Reconnect existing Sixel/iTerm atlas graphics to the renderer's current image-overlay path using in-house helper code; reuse existing GraphicDataEntry and GraphicOverlay storage rather than adding a parallel renderer. Keep terminal child identity truthful by advertising Rio capabilities through TERM/TERM_PROGRAM while exposing a Yazelix-specific host marker separately. Rejected alternatives: restoring the retired rich-text graphics pipeline, copying Kitty implementation code, adding an image/rendering crate, or forcing app-specific Yazi configuration. Packaging impact: none for this experiment repo.
close_reason: Completed: validated the Yazelix stack under target/debug/rio --yazelix with WGPU/GL. Fixed atlas Sixel/iTerm rendering by bridging atlas graphics into the renderer image-overlay path, fixed Kitty U=1 virtual-placement rendering by inferring omitted c/r dimensions from image and cell metrics, corrected virtual source rectangles to the renderer shader shape, and cleaned Yazelix host child terminal identity. Verified focused rioterm and rio-backend tests, WGPU build, conformance verify, git diff --check, direct Sixel screenshot, direct Kitty unicode-placeholder screenshot, Yazi Kitty preview through Zellij/Yazelix, and Helix-in-stack screenshot evidence.

title: Implement OSC 133 semantic prompts and command zones
description: Bring shell semantic prompt/command zone behavior to Ghostty-grade parity. Include parser behavior, rendering/selection policy, and shell integration validation.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new production crates. Build OSC 133 parsing in-house in rio-backend's existing typed OSC parser because the grammar is small and existing helpers already parse separator-split OSC payloads. Store terminal semantic state in existing grid rows and Crosswords state instead of adding a frontend protocol bus. Rejected alternatives: importing Ghostty parser code because it is Zig/C-facing and not a Rust dependency boundary; using a broad parser crate because key=value semicolon parsing is trivial; adding per-cell semantic metadata now because row-level prompt/input/output zones are enough for prompt navigation and selection boundaries and avoid expanding the hot 8-byte Square representation. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 133 semantic prompt foundation: typed parser for A/B/C/D/I/L/N/P actions and Ghostty/Kitty options, production OSC dispatch to Handler::semantic_prompt, durable row-level semantic zones for prompt, prompt continuation, input, and output, and tests for parser, real byte-stream dispatch, and Crosswords row state. Verified cargo fmt --check, rio-backend semantic_prompt tests with platform features, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features. Created a follow-up for prompt navigation and command-output selection actions on top of this state.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 66 text sizing
description: Implement Kitty text sizing support if absent or incomplete. Validate against Kitty spec and Ghostty behavior without copying Kitty GPL implementation code.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement the OSC 66 parser in rio-backend's existing OSC helper layer and implement the first parity slice in Crosswords by honoring the declared width metadata during grid insertion. Build rendering scale metadata in-house only if it can fit existing Row/Square side-table ownership without widening Square. Rejected alternatives: copying Kitty's GPL implementation; adding HarfBuzz or osc66 as a dependency because terminal-side OSC 66 support consumes explicit client metadata and does not need to shape text itself; implementing full multi-size renderer first because width support is the protocol-detection and complex-script correctness gate. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the OSC 66 protocol foundation and width support: typed parser for scale, declared width, fractional scale, alignment, and text payloads; production OSC dispatch to Handler::input_sized_text; Crosswords insertion that advances by client-declared width and scale for protocol detection and complex-script width control; and focused parser, byte-stream dispatch, and cursor-advance tests. Verified text_sizing and osc66 rio-backend tests with platform features, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features. Created a follow-up for full visual scale/fraction rendering.

title: Harden clipboard notification and identity protocols
description: Close protocol gaps around OSC 52 clipboard policy, OSC 9/777/99 notifications, XTGETTCAP/DA/XTVERSION, terminal identity, and terminfo behavior.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement Kitty OSC 99 parsing in rio-backend's OSC helper layer and reuse the existing RioEvent::DesktopNotification path for display. Use existing simd_base64 and simd_utf8 helpers for encoded payloads. Build a small in-house pending notification map for chunked title/body payloads; do not copy Kitty GPL code. Rejected alternatives: adding a notification protocol crate, implementing OS notification lifecycle callbacks before parser/display support, or changing frontend notification policy before the backend supports the protocol. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the first protocol-hardening slice: Kitty OSC 99 parser for metadata/title/body/base64 payloads, production dispatch through Handler::kitty_notification, Crosswords chunked title/body accumulation by notification id, reuse of existing DesktopNotification event, OSC 99 conformance fixture, and follow-up beads for OSC 52/identity truthfulness plus OSC 99 lifecycle replies. Verified kitty_notification tests, cargo fmt --check, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Adopt parser fuzz and robustness strategy
description: Study Ghostty/WezTerm parser robustness practices and add a practical Rust/Rio fuzz or fixture strategy for OSC/CSI/APC edge cases.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates for the first robustness slice. Add deterministic in-tree parser fuzz-smoke coverage using a tiny LCG and the existing parser test dispatcher so CI can run it everywhere. Document when to graduate to cargo-fuzz/libFuzzer if parser bugs appear or corpus minimization becomes important. Rejected alternatives: adding proptest/quickcheck just for byte noise, adding cargo-fuzz before there is a stable corpus boundary, or shelling out to external fuzzers in default checks. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed parser robustness first slice: added deterministic dependency-free parser noise smoke coverage, documented the fuzzing escalation strategy, and verified with cargo fmt, focused rio-backend parser test, conformance fixture verification, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check.

title: Benchmark performance and graphics stability against Ghostty
description: Measure startup, frame stability, shader overhead, scrollback/render behavior, and graphics workloads against Ghostty where practical. Record repeatable local commands and evidence.
close_reason: Completed local release-build benchmark pass against Ghostty 1.3.1. Added docs/yazelix/performance_and_graphics_benchmark.md with controlled startup/exit timings, scrollback stress smoke timings, screenshots, raw CSV artifacts, methodology, and limitations. Added artifacts/benchmarks/startup_exit_2026_05_31.csv, artifacts/benchmarks/scroll_stress_2026_05_31.csv, artifacts/benchmarks/screenshots/ghostty_shader_probe.png, and the no-shader Rio WGPU config used for comparison. Linked the evidence from the Ghostty parity contract and filed yzt-7p3.39 for deeper frame-time instrumentation. Verified release build, benchmark commands, python stats readback, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate frontier Kitty protocols
description: Evaluate Kitty multiple cursors, file transfer, drag/drop, arbitrary-region styling, and other frontier extensions after core protocols and shader parity are stable.
notes: Outcome documented in docs/yazelix/frontier_kitty_protocols.md. Accepted immediate Ghostty-parity protocol gaps: OSC 21 color control, OSC 5522 rich clipboard, and end-to-end OSC 22 pointer shapes. Accepted should/frontier follow-ups: Kitty multiple cursors, keyboard completeness audit, unscrolling CSI +T, DECCARA all-SGR region styling, file-transfer security-policy design, and OSC 72 drag/drop tracking. Existing beads already cover OSC 133 prompt actions, OSC 66 visual rendering, OSC 99 lifecycle, and WGPU Vulkan validation.
close_reason: Documented frontier Kitty protocol classification and created follow-up beads yzt-7p3.23 through yzt-7p3.31 for accepted protocol work.

title: Fix WGPU launch and shader screenshot validation environment
description: Resolve or bypass the local WGPU/native graphics launch blocker so Ghostty-compatible custom shaders can be visually validated with screenshots. Current failures: native Vulkan ERROR_INCOMPATIBLE_DRIVER; WGPU/GL incompatible_surface_backends; WGPU/Vulkan CreateSurfaceError; host launch outside Nix missing Wayland/X11 dynamic helper libraries. This should produce a reliable launch recipe or packaging fix, then capture the Ghostty cursor probe screenshot.
notes: Working hypothesis: prefer a reproducible launch wrapper over host-specific fixes. First try Nix-provided graphics wrappers and backend/env combinations; only mutate host packages if the missing dependency is clear and the command is non-destructive. Success requires a screenshot artifact from the Ghostty cursor shader probe.
close_reason: Resolved the shader screenshot validation blocker by passing Sugarloaf's raw display handle into wgpu::InstanceDescriptor, adding a repeatable launch-wgpu-shader-screenshot harness command, and capturing artifacts/shader_probe/screenshots/wgpu_shader_probe_gl.png. Verified WGPU/GL launch with Ghostty cursor probe, py_compile, rioterm cargo check --features wgpu, sugarloaf ghostty shader tests with Linux window features, conformance verify, and git diff --check. WGPU/Vulkan remains tracked separately as yzt-7p3.22.

title: Add OSC 133 prompt navigation and selection actions
description: Build on the row-level OSC 133 prompt/input/output metadata by adding user-visible prompt navigation and command-output selection behavior. This should cover previous/next prompt movement, command-output region selection policy, and any Yazelix shell UX that depends on semantic prompt boundaries.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Reuse existing OSC 133 row metadata, grid/search primitives, selection range types, and frontend action/keybinding surfaces. Implement prompt navigation and output-range selection as small in-house helpers. Rejected alternatives: adding a shell-integration library, introducing a generalized semantic-region index before profiling, or depending on Ghostty internals. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 133 prompt navigation and command-output selection actions. Added backend navigation/selection regressions, config action parsing coverage, and docs update. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend semantic_ tests; rioterm semantic_prompt_actions_parse_from_config_strings; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Render OSC 66 scale and fractional text visually
description: Build on the OSC 66 parser and width-aware grid insertion by carrying scale/fraction/alignment metadata into the renderer and drawing larger/smaller text spans. Keep the packed Square representation small; use side-table metadata only if the renderer can consume it without broad grid churn.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 66 visual rendering slice. Text sizing metadata now lives in the Extras side table, anchor cells carry TEXT_SIZING flags, and the grid emitter renders scaled/fractional text runs from that payload without growing Square. Added backend metadata regression coverage, updated parity docs, and created yzt-7p3.37 for the remaining full multicell editing semantics. Verified with cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, conformance verify, focused rio-backend text_sizing tests, rioterm cargo check, cargo build -p rioterm, and a local CPU-renderer OSC 66 screenshot probe.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Rust dependency gate: use in-house/std only. Existing OSC 66 parsing and rendering data paths are enough for this slice; no production or dev-only crates are needed. Rejected adding shaping/layout crates because the terminal already owns glyph layout, and the bead is about carrying parsed metadata into the existing renderer. Packaging impact: none.

title: Define Ghostty parity contract
description: Define must/should/frontier parity for Ghostty behavior. Include cursor shader semantics, protocols, terminal identity, shell integration, security/source-absorption policy, validation evidence, and pivot criteria if Rio cannot support the target cleanly.
close_reason: Completed Ghostty parity contract covering cursor shader semantics, protocol tiers, Yazelix mode boundaries, validation evidence, absorption rules, and pivot criteria.

title: Audit OSC 52 policy and terminal identity truthfulness
description: Review OSC 52 clipboard read/write policy, DA/XTVERSION terminal identity, XTGETTCAP capability claims, and packaged terminfo for truthfulness against the protocols implemented in yazelix-terminal. Add policy gates or corrections where Rio currently advertises or allows behavior too broadly.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Harden OSC 52 using existing simd_base64/simd_utf8 helpers and existing RioEvent clipboard events. Add explicit size and decode policy in Crosswords rather than introducing a frontend clipboard policy framework. Audit identity via existing XTGETTCAP table and TERM_PROGRAM/Yazelix-mode environment. Rejected alternatives: adding a prompt/permission UI before protocol state is correct, changing TERM away from Rio's terminfo names before packaged terminfo is audited, or advertising unverified capabilities. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Audited and hardened the non-interactive OSC 52/identity slice: OSC 52 stores now reject unsupported clipboard designators, invalid base64, non-UTF-8 payloads, and oversized encoded/decoded payloads with visible warnings; added backend regression coverage; documented current TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/XTVERSION/terminfo truthfulness and remaining policy gaps. Verified osc52 backend test, cargo fmt, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Complete OSC 99 lifecycle replies and activation behavior
description: Extend the initial OSC 99 display/chunking support with alive/query replies, close/update lifecycle behavior where the frontend can observe OS notification state, and optional activation/focus reply handling if supported by Rio's notifier backend.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: add no new crates. Use the existing OSC 99 parser, Crosswords pending notification state, RioEvent PTY write route, and desktop notification event path. Implement alive/query replies and close/update bookkeeping in-house against the Kitty desktop notification spec. Rejected alternatives: depending on desktop notification libraries for lifecycle feedback before Rio's notifier backend exposes IDs, adding a permission UI for notifications, or broadening this bead to full button/icon activation. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Implemented the deterministic OSC 99 lifecycle protocol slice: sanitized notification IDs before replies, parsed c=1 close-report metadata, tracked live notification IDs for p=alive replies, answered p=? support queries, honored p=close in backend state, and emitted p=close;untracked when close reports are requested but Rio cannot observe OS close events. Created follow-up yzt-0o4 for OS-backed notification handles, update/replace, activation, button, and true close callbacks. Verified cargo fmt --check; rio-backend kitty_notification tests; python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify; git diff --check; nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm. Only observed warning is the preexisting rio-window X11 IME function-cast warning.

title: Fix WGPU Vulkan surface creation on COSMIC NVIDIA
description: WGPU shader validation now works through WGPU_BACKEND=gl, but WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan still fails on this COSMIC Wayland/NVIDIA host with CreateSurfaceError { inner: Hal(FailedToCreateSurfaceForAnyBackend({})) }. Investigate whether this is a wgpu/raw-window-handle integration issue, a Nix/NVIDIA wrapper issue, or a compositor/backend limitation. Keep WGPU/GL as the validation recipe until this is fixed.
close_reason: Current tree no longer reproduces the COSMIC Wayland/NVIDIA WGPU/Vulkan CreateSurfaceError. Verified the exact shader screenshot harness with its new default WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan, captured visual evidence at artifacts/shader_probe/screenshots/wgpu_shader_probe_vulkan.png, kept GL as explicit fallback, and updated docs. Also verified python3 -m py_compile tools/yazelix_conformance.py, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, and git diff --check.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 21 color control
description: Implement Kitty OSC 21 keyed color control for Yazelix-terminal. Scope: parse set/query/reset packets; support foreground, background, cursor, cursor_text, selection foreground/background, visual_bell where representable, transparent_background_color slots where practical, and ANSI palette entries; reply with key=? for unknown query fields; preserve existing OSC 10/11/12 and palette reset behavior; add conformance fixtures and focused tests. Reference Kitty official docs and Ghostty's MIT OSC 21 parser/behavior for parity, without copying Kitty implementation code.
notes: Implemented no-new-crate first slice: OSC 21 parser, set/query/reset dispatch, foreground/background/cursor/palette mapping, rgbi plus small named color parsing for Ghostty's aliceblue fixture, unsupported query reply as key=?, conformance fixture, and focused tests. Harder remaining gap: cursor_text, selection colors, visual_bell, and transparent background need terminal/front-end storage and possibly batched OSC21 replies; filed yzt-03p for that continuation.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 21 first slice: typed parser, set/query/reset dispatch, foreground/background/cursor/palette support, unsupported query reply, conformance fixture, docs, and focused tests. Filed yzt-03p for special-color storage and batched query continuation.

title: Implement Kitty OSC 5522 rich clipboard
description: Implement a safe first slice of Kitty OSC 5522 rich clipboard support. Scope: parse metadata/payload packets; support type=read, type=write, type=wdata, and type=walias for text/plain first; route reads/writes through an explicit visible policy like the hardened OSC 52 path; fail closed on unsupported MIME, invalid base64, oversized chunks, malformed sessions, and missing permissions; add parser, policy, and conformance tests. Use Kitty docs as the protocol source and Ghostty's MIT parser as a behavior reference.
close_reason: Implemented OSC 5522 text/plain read/write/wdata/walias first slice with parser, policy events, conformance fixture, tests, and docs. Deferred arbitrary rich MIME and password trust prompts as future work.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Crate-vs-in-house decision: use in-house/std plus existing base64/UTF-8 helpers. The protocol grammar is a small key-value metadata parser and payload gate that should live near existing OSC parsing; adding a URL/querystring parser or MIME crate would not buy enough and could weaken fail-closed handling. Reuse existing base64, simd_utf8, ClipboardType, RioEvent ClipboardLoad/Store/PtyWrite paths. Packaging impact: none.

comments.author: lucca

Each accepted feature needs threat-model docs and tests.
notes: Crate decision: no new production or dev crates for this policy slice. Use existing in-house OSC 5113 parser/manager, std::fs metadata/path APIs, and current tempfile-based tests. Rejected new crates because the remaining work is protocol policy, explicit fail-closed behavior, and regression coverage; no packaging impact.
close_reason: Completed OSC 5113 full-spec policy slice. Added regressions proving pw/shared-secret metadata does not bypass approval, symlink/hardlink file types are rejected without creating staged files, and rsync/delta transmission rejects before sessions/prompts. Updated file-transfer policy, frontier protocol notes, and Ghostty parity contract to mark destination chooser UX, link handling, metadata preservation, scoped bypass, and rsync/delta as explicit policy rejections until separate trust/test slices exist. Verified cargo fmt --check, focused osc5113 tests, kitty_file_transfer parser tests, cargo check -p rioterm, conformance verify, and git diff --check.

Keep Helix/Yazi/Zellij workflows stable.

Use existing rio-window keyboard state/event types, in-house Kitty CSI-u encoder, and existing black-box conformance harness/docs. title: Implement or explicitly defer Kitty OSC 72 drag-and-drop runtime
description: Move beyond the tracking-policy bead for Kitty OSC 72. Implement support only if the spec and OS event routing are mature enough; otherwise keep runtime support unadvertised and document the exact external blockers. Scope includes support query behavior, OS drag/drop routing, MIME negotiation, chunking, remote-machine semantics, and security prompts.
notes: Crate decision: no new crates. This slice is an explicit defer/no-runtime-support decision backed by the official Kitty OSC 72 warning and local rio-window event audit. Existing OS file-drop paths only expose path-level HoveredFile/DroppedFile events on some backends, without MIME lists, pixel/cell movement, data request handles, source-drag offers, same-window origin tracking, or Wayland coverage. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Explicitly deferred OSC 72 runtime support with current evidence. Re-checked the official Kitty DnD spec on 2026-05-31; it still warns the protocol is under development. Audited local rio-window drop boundaries: Windows/macOS/X11 expose path-level HoveredFile/DroppedFile-style events, but not MIME lists, per-move cell/pixel coordinates, chunked data request handles, drag-source offers, final operation reporting, same-window source/drop denial, or Wayland coverage. Updated DnD policy, frontier notes, and parity blockers to keep t=q unadvertised until those prerequisites exist. Verified conformance verify and git diff --check.

title: Throttle shader/game redraw loop
description: The comparable 2026-05-31 shader idle benchmark found Rio WGPU shader/game mode presenting 69,688 frames over ~4s with ~97% CPU, while Ghostty's shader run completed the same idle workload at ~18% sampled process CPU. Investigate whether Rio's game render strategy is bypassing vblank/throttling, then cap redraws to display cadence or the minimum animation requirement without breaking cursor shader animation.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision: no new crates. This is event-loop scheduling/render throttling in existing Rio/router/window code, so use std::time and existing vblank_interval/request_redraw machinery. Rejected external timing/GPU crates because the bug is visible in current frame metrics and should be fixed at the redraw ownership boundary. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Fixed shader/game redraw hot loop. Replaced immediate game-mode request_redraw + ControlFlow::Poll with a GameRender scheduler tick at the window vblank interval, while preserving non-game pre_present_notify handling. Release benchmark evidence: Rio WGPU shader idle dropped from 69,688 presented frames/~96.9% sampled CPU to 237 presented frames/~2.5% sampled CPU over the same 4s workload, with 16.99ms median frame delta. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p rioterm, cargo build -p rioterm --release, throttled frame-run benchmark, benchmark py_compile/self-test, conformance verify, and git diff --check.

title: Create conformance and screenshot harness

title: Prove Rio renderer can host Ghostty cursor shader parity

close_reason: Completed renderer feasibility proof. WGPU build passes; source audit shows Rio already has cursor/palette/focus state plus a WGPU postprocess seam suitable for a dedicated GhosttyShaderBrush. title: Implement Ghostty-compatible cursor shader runtime
description: Implement the runtime/config path for Ghostty-style custom cursor shaders after the renderer feasibility decision is positive. Include shader loading, uniforms, timing, errors, tests, and a visual validation artifact.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: production crates remain on Rio's existing WGPU stack. Use existing wgpu 29 with the glsl feature enabled for Ghostty/Shadertoy fragment modules, existing bytemuck for uniform POD packing, existing tracing for visible shader load/render errors, and existing serde config plumbing. Do not add a new shader framework. Do not use Kitty implementation code. Build in-house Sugarloaf GhosttyShaderBrush beside FiltersBrush. Rejected alternatives: overload librashader filters because RetroArch presets do not expose Ghostty cursor uniforms; add a broad shader abstraction crate because WGPU already supports GLSL modules with naga; implement native Vulkan/Metal first because WGPU is the smallest existing postprocess seam. Packaging impact: WGPU builds already pay most dependencies, adding wgpu/glsl enables naga glsl-in for the WGPU feature path.

title: Implement Yazelix mode without terminal-native workspace ownership
description: Add a Yazelix mode that runs one child command and lets Zellij own panes/tabs/workspace behavior. Hide or disable Rio tabs/splits/navigation in Yazelix mode unless explicitly running standalone.
notes: Dependency decision before Rust work: use no new crates. Implement Yazelix mode in the existing clap CLI/config plumbing because Rio already has a single child command surface through -e/--command. Build the mode in-house by applying conservative config overrides: require a command, disable Rio split keybinding/config paths, default the app id to yazelix-terminal, and set TERM_PROGRAM accordingly. Rejected alternatives: adding a second launcher syntax, deleting Rio split code in the first slice, or introducing a Yazelix-specific config format before validating runtime behavior. close_reason: Implemented the initial Yazelix mode CLI surface: --yazelix requires -e/--command, defaults app id to yazelix-terminal, sets TERM_PROGRAM to yazelix-terminal, disables Rio native split keybindings/config-editor split ownership, and documents the intended launch contract. Verified rioterm yazelix_mode tests, cargo fmt --check, conformance verify, git diff --check, and rioterm cargo check with platform features.

title: Stop LD_LIBRARY_PATH leak into child shells
description: Yazelix Terminal's Nix wrapper sets a private LD_LIBRARY_PATH for Rio runtime libraries. Child shells and commands launched from the terminal must not inherit that private Nix library path, because system binaries can then load Nix glibc/runtime libraries and fail. Audit the PTY and command spawn paths and make child environment sanitization reliable for default shells, -e commands, Yazelix desktop launches, and respawn/helper paths. Acceptance: from a fresh Yazelix Terminal launch, child env LD_LIBRARY_PATH is empty or contains only preserved host/user entries, not terminal rlinkLibs; YAZELIX_TERMINAL_* launch-only env vars do not leak into child shells; a system GUI tool such as zenity can be launched without mixed Nix/system glibc errors; regression tests cover wrapper-provided stripping, host LD_LIBRARY_PATH preservation, and launch-only env removal.
notes: Evidence update: current installed package did not reproduce LD_LIBRARY_PATH leakage for direct -e children or the desktop wrapper -e path when launched with a clean host LD_LIBRARY_PATH; both child env captures lacked LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Code audit found a narrower real gap: direct frontend helper spawns such as the docs URL opener bypassed teletypewriter's child env sanitizer and would inherit Rio's process-wide wrapper LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Fix hardens that path and exposes the sanitizer for frontend helper commands while preserving the existing PTY spawn/fork cleanup behavior.
close_reason: Fixed and hardened. Current installed package did not reproduce LD_LIBRARY_PATH leakage for direct or desktop-wrapper -e PTY children, which suggests the broad child-shell leak was from an older live session or another layer. Code audit found direct frontend helper spawns bypassing the sanitizer, so the teletypewriter sanitizer is now a public Unix helper and the docs URL launcher uses it; internal ps and Flatpak shell probes are sanitized too. Added regression coverage for empty host LD_LIBRARY_PATH snapshots, host LD_LIBRARY_PATH preservation, and launch-only env removal. Validation: cargo fmt -- --check; cargo test -p teletypewriter yazelix_child_command; cargo check -p rioterm; cargo build -p rioterm; live target/debug/rio env captures for empty-host and preserved-host LD_LIBRARY_PATH; git diff --check.

title: Clamp cursor trail for TUI scroll jumps
description:
Screenshots from a live Yazelix session show large orange cursor-trail wedges while scrolling Codex, Helix, and Yazi through Zellij. The same artifact across apps means the terminal cursor effect is following terminal-cursor redraw jumps rather than only intentional nearby cursor movement.

Current evidence:
- Packaged config enables WGPU Ghostty-style cursor shaders and effects.trail-cursor.
- frontends/rioterm/src/renderer/trail_cursor.rs treats only tiny horizontal moves as short; one-row vertical j/k movement receives the full 150 ms spring.
- Large TUI redraw jumps are animated instead of snapped, producing screen-spanning trail quads.
- sugarloaf Ghostty shader uniforms also preserve previous/current cursor rectangles, so large jumps can feed shader trails.

This should be fixed in yzxterm/Rio rendering policy. Zellij is a good trigger/repro harness but should not need a patch unless terminal-side isolation fails.
acceptance_criteria:
Small nearby cursor movement can still animate.
One-row vertical editor/file-manager movement uses short animation timing.
Cursor jumps larger than a conservative cell threshold snap instead of drawing screen-spanning trails.
Ghostty shader previous-cursor uniforms do not expose huge cursor jumps to shader trails.
Focused tests cover short vertical movement and large-jump snapping.
Local validation includes cargo fmt/check/test for the touched crates.
close_reason: Completed. Patched yzxterm cursor effects so nearby vertical TUI movement uses short animation, redraw-scale jumps snap instead of drawing screen-spanning trails, and Ghostty shader uniforms do not expose huge previous/current cursor jumps. Focused rioterm and sugarloaf tests plus WGPU rioterm check pass.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Implemented terminal-side cursor trail clamping.

Findings:
- This is yzxterm/Rio rendering policy, not a Zellij patch target. Zellij/Codex/Helix/Yazi only trigger the issue by moving the terminal cursor during redraws.
- The solid orange diagonal comes from the CPU trail cursor drawing a large animated quad. One-row vertical motion previously used the full 150 ms animation because only tiny horizontal moves were considered short.
- The WGPU Ghostty shader path also preserved previous/current cursor rectangles, so huge redraw jumps could feed GLSL cursor trails.

Changes:
- Nearby cursor moves up to about two cells use the short 40 ms animation, including vertical editor/file-manager movement.
- Cursor jumps over eight cells snap instead of animating.
- Shader uniforms seed first-frame previous cursor to current and snap previous=current on huge jumps.

Validation:
- cargo fmt -- --check
- cargo test -p rioterm trail_cursor
- cargo test -p rioterm --features wgpu trail_cursor
- cargo test -p sugarloaf --features "wgpu rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen" cursor
- cargo check -p rioterm --features wgpu
- git diff --check

title: Desktop launcher needs graphics wrapper on non-NixOS
description: The COSMIC app-library path does invoke yazelix-terminal.desktop, but desktop-launched Rio crashes before opening with native Vulkan: vkCreateInstance ERROR_INCOMPATIBLE_DRIVER. Shell launches work because Yazelix carries graphics driver env/nixGL. Add a packaged desktop launcher wrapper that uses an explicit graphics wrapper when available, so the app-library path works outside an already-initialized Yazelix shell.
close_reason: Added a packaged yazelix-terminal-desktop launcher that finds the Yazelix/nixGL graphics wrapper before executing Rio, wired the generated desktop entry through it, rebuilt .#yazelix-terminal, validated both desktop files, refreshed the local launcher entry, and confirmed COSMIC launcher selection opens a rendered terminal window.

title: Wire Yazelix cursor shader presets into Yazelix Terminal
description:
The packaged Yazelix Terminal window accepts input after renderer.strategy = "game", but the Yazelix Ghostty cursor preset stack is not visible: cursor trail and cursor widget effects do not render. Investigate config discovery, packaged/default config, WGPU-only shader requirements, native vs WGPU backend selection, shader path resolution, and Yazelix launch env propagation.

Acceptance:
- Reproduce with the desktop entry or packaged wrapper.
- Prove whether [renderer].custom-shader is absent, ignored, using the wrong backend, or failing shader compilation/loading.
- Make failures visible instead of silently disabling cursor effects.
- Validate with screenshot evidence using at least one Yazelix cursor trail preset and the cursor widget preset.
notes:
Investigation evidence 2026-05-31:
- Running desktop process had RIO_CONFIG_HOME=/nix/store/.../share/yazelix-terminal, and generated config only set confirm-before-quit plus renderer.strategy = "game". No custom-shader entries were loaded.
- The installed/unwrapped package was built with --no-default-features --features=x11,wayland; the WGPU-only Ghostty shader code is compiled out. Strings in the binary did not expose the Ghostty shader error/config surface.
- Cursor shader parity therefore needs a package/config fix, not a runtime Yazelix setting tweak: build a shader-capable package with feature wgpu, select backend = "Webgpu" when shaders are configured, and load Yazelix Ghostty cursor preset paths from the packaged/runtime config surface with visible errors on failure.
close_reason: Completed. Packaged Yazelix Terminal now builds with the WGPU feature enabled, installs the Yazelix Ghostty-style cursor shader assets into share/yazelix-terminal/shaders, substitutes those absolute paths into the packaged config, selects backend = "Webgpu", and enables trail-cursor. The explicit game-mode wrapper path now injects strategy = "game" into the existing renderer table without losing shader config. Validation: cargo fmt --check, cargo test --features wgpu ghostty, cargo check -p rioterm --no-default-features --features wgpu,x11,wayland, alejandra --check, nix build .#yazelix-terminal-fast, tools/yazelix_event_mode_smoke.sh ./result_yazelix_terminal_fast_package. Manual evidence: packaged event-mode window opened and maintainer confirmed shader was visible while typing.

title: Render OSC 21 visual-bell and transparent-background effects
description: Implement actual rendering behavior for OSC 21 visual_bell and transparent_background after the special color slots are stored/queryable. Visual bell likely needs a real Sugarloaf overlay/animation path tied to Bell events; transparent_background needs a compositor/window-background policy so it does not fight configured opacity, images, or per-panel OSC 11 backgrounds.
notes: Dependency decision: use in-house/std only. The visual-bell path is renderer timing plus an existing Sugarloaf rect primitive, and transparent-background composition is existing color state plus window-background policy. Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Rendered OSC 21 visual effects: BEL triggers a fading overlay colored by visual_bell, second_transparent_background/transparent_background drives transparent-window composition when no background image is active, Ghostty key alias is parsed, docs/conformance updated. Verified: cargo fmt --check; rioterm visual_bell test; rioterm transparent_background test; rio-backend second_transparent test; conformance verify; git diff --check; cargo check -p rioterm; cargo build -p rioterm.

title: Document Cargo inner-loop workflow before Nix packaging gates
close_reason: Documented the intended build-speed workflow: enter nix develop once, use Cargo directly for Rust check/build/run, use yazelix-terminal-fast for local package/launcher smoke, and reserve the checked package for release gates. Verified nix develop --command cargo check -p rioterm, cached fast/full Nix builds, README link, and git diff --check.

title: Yazi PDF preview can freeze Rio render loop
description: Previewing a PDF from Yazi inside the debug yazelix-terminal/Rio session froze the terminal. Process evidence after the freeze: no live pdftoppm/magick/mutool/ffmpeg preview subprocess remained; the affected Yazi process was idle on futex/epoll; the debug Rio process stayed alive at roughly 30-40% CPU until killed. Suspect a terminal-side graphics/render loop issue after Yazi emits a PDF-derived image preview, likely through Kitty graphics or a related image path. Reproduce with Yazi PDF preview inside yazelix-terminal, capture terminal logs, and add a regression around the image/protocol path once isolated.
close_reason: Resolved by the image overlay source-rect fix and validated by maintainer retest: Yazi image and PDF previews no longer freeze the terminal.

title: Add true fast maintainer package for terminal iteration
description:
Create a maintainer-only Yazelix Terminal package profile that rebuilds much faster than the release package. The current fast package still uses the expensive optimized Rio link path, so Home Manager testing remains slow after small Rust changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add or adjust a flake package/app for maintainer iteration, separate from the default user package.
- Disable the expensive release-only knobs for that package, such as LTO, split debuginfo, release checks, or other slow flags where safe.
- Keep the default user package optimized and unchanged unless measurement proves otherwise.
- Document that the fast package is for maintainer smoke testing, not the final shipped runtime.
- Measure and record build time before/after on a warm local rebuild after a small source edit.

title: Make event-based rendering correct on Wayland/COSMIC
description: Remove the need for packaged renderer.strategy = "game" as the default Wayland/COSMIC responsiveness workaround. Rio/Yazelix Terminal event-based rendering should wake and present correctly after idle PTY output, keyboard input/echo, resize, Zellij pane actions, and explicit animation frames. Acceptance: default/event strategy passes the idle input, resize, Zellij pane creation/navigation, and Yazi preview smoke tests without continuous game-mode redraws; packaged config can stop defaulting to game mode.
notes: Manual verification 2026-06-01: fresh Home Manager-installed Yazelix Terminal window with event strategy default, decorations disabled, and fonts.size=18.0 feels fast and visually good. Maintainer confirmed typing/session behavior after the package/default changes. Remaining open work is yzt-ff9.4: a repeatable event-mode responsiveness harness before closing the epic.

The package defaults to event mode, the wrapper still offers explicit game override, idle/input/resize/Zellij/Yazi behavior has maintainer manual smoke coverage, and cursor shader/trail animation now has event-mode redraw scheduling plus smoke documentation.

title: Diagnose event-mode idle wake failures
description: Build a focused diagnosis pass for default/event strategy on Wayland/COSMIC. Capture whether PTY damage, input, resize, EventLoopProxy wakeups, request_redraw, frame callbacks, or RedrawRequested delivery fails after idle. Acceptance: a short investigation note in the bead identifies the failing stage with source paths and a minimal repro command or harness.
notes: Diagnosis 2026-05-31: the default/event strategy can strand Wayland frame callbacks. In frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs, WindowEvent::RedrawRequested calls route.window.winit_window.pre_present_notify() before route.window.screen.render(). In frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs, Screen::render() may decide should_present=false and call sugarloaf.discard_frame() instead of committing/presenting. On Wayland, pre_present_notify() maps to rio-window/src/platform_impl/linux/wayland/window/mod.rs -> WindowState::request_frame_callback(), which sets FrameCallbackState::Requested. If no commit follows, rio-window/src/platform_impl/linux/wayland/event_loop/mod.rs later short-circuits redraw delivery while the state remains Requested. That matches the user-visible idle/freeze shape: input/PTY/resize can request redraw, but RedrawRequested is suppressed until an external event path unsticks the state. Minimal repro path: packaged desktop with renderer.strategy=event on Wayland/COSMIC, launch yzx enter, let the terminal idle, then trigger Zellij pane navigation/creation or Yazi preview redraw; the failure stage is frame callback / RedrawRequested delivery, not PTY writes.
close_reason: Diagnosed the event-mode idle failure as a Wayland frame callback ordering bug: pre_present_notify() can set FrameCallbackState::Requested before Screen::render() decides not to present, and the Wayland event loop suppresses later RedrawRequested delivery while that callback is still requested.

title: Fix Wayland event-loop wake and redraw delivery
description: Fix the event-based path so terminal/user events wake ControlFlow::Wait and reliably produce a presented frame on Wayland after idle. Likely source areas: frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs and rio-window/src/platform_impl/linux/wayland/event_loop/mod.rs. Acceptance: event strategy responds to idle keyboard input, PTY output, resize, and Zellij pane actions without renderer.strategy = "game".
close_reason: Fixed event-mode Wayland frame callback ordering by moving pre_present_notify into the actual present path. Built yazelix-terminal-fast and user confirmed launcher typing works under event strategy.

title: Decouple animation redraws from global game mode
description: Make cursor shaders, cursor trails, visual bell, scrollbar animation, and other explicit animations schedule paced redraws only while active, without requiring renderer.strategy = "game" for the entire terminal. Acceptance: animation surfaces continue to animate under event strategy, idle terminal sessions sleep, and no path forces full terminal damage merely because an animation scheduler is active.
notes: Implemented the first animation decoupling slice: event mode records renderer.needs_redraw() after a presented frame and schedules a plain RioEvent::Render at vblank instead of forcing RenderRoute/full terminal grid damage. Keep open until cursor shader/trail behavior is manually validated under event strategy.

title: Investigate Helix input latency regression versus upstream Rio
description:
Problem:
Vanilla upstream Rio opened from the terminal feels substantially smoother than yazelix-terminal when spamming Helix j/k movement. This suggests the fork's event/render scheduling, cursor effects, Yazelix mode, or wrapper/runtime environment may have regressed interactive input latency or frame pacing relative to Rio itself.

Scope:
- Compare fork event/render/input scheduling against upstream Rio, especially changes made for the event-system fix and cursor/effects redraw paths.
- Evaluate whether rolling back the event-system fix would restore Helix responsiveness, whether the fix should instead be narrowed, or whether a different scheduling patch is needed.
- Build a reproducible Helix j/k benchmark or manual protocol using vanilla Rio and yazelix-terminal under comparable config/env.
- Identify the smallest safe patch direction before touching main Yazelix integration.

Acceptance criteria:
- Source paths and likely regression mechanisms are documented with code references.
- Rollback versus patch tradeoff is explicitly evaluated.
- A proposed way forward names the preferred patch shape, verification commands, and risk to Rio #1603-style resize/repaint behavior.
- Findings are linked back to upstreaming bead yzt-upstream-event-system-fixes-tg9 if they affect whether the event-system fix should be proposed upstream.
notes:
Investigation snapshot, 2026-06-01

Current conclusion
- Do not rollback the event-system fix as the first response to the Helix j/k latency report. The user's observation compares vanilla upstream Rio, likely with no Yazelix shader/trail defaults, against yazelix-terminal's packaged visual defaults. That is not yet a clean event-system A/B.
- The most likely immediate regression mechanism is packaged effects cost under event mode: WebGPU + three full-screen Ghostty-style shaders + trail cursor animation. The event-system fix may expose that cost because it keeps shader/trail animation alive without global game mode, but the expensive path appears to be the effect stack rather than the pre_present_notify fix itself.

Source evidence
- Fork is currently based on upstream Rio main at 7e18dde1c90182a5170a7cca7779544967d7291c. The event-system fix is local commit 6702381d48 ("Fix event-mode Wayland redraw scheduling").
- Event fix shape:
  - frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs:2013-2019 passes pre_present_notify into screen.render only for non-game terminal frames.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs:2121-2135 schedules event-mode animation redraws at vblank when last_render_needs_redraw is true.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs:2137-2149 still requests redraw when pending terminal/UI damage remains dirty.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs:4478-4497 computes renderer/shader animation needs and only presents when panel damage or animation requires it.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs:4531-4540 marks pending_update dirty for the next vsync when animation needs redraw.
- Packaged yazelix-terminal default config is not vanilla Rio:
  - misc/yazelix_terminal_config.toml:3-9 selects WebGPU and three custom shaders.
  - misc/yazelix_terminal_config.toml:14-15 enables trail-cursor.
- Ghostty shader redraw pressure:
  - sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs:13 sets SHADER_ANIMATION_WINDOW to 650 ms.
  - sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs:248-260 re-arms that window whenever GhosttyShaderFrameState changes; Helix j/k moves the cursor, so spam can keep this continuously active.
  - sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs:295-344 allocates/copies a source texture, allocates intermediate textures for stacked shaders, and runs one full-screen pass per shader every shader frame.
  - sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs:610-629 creates texture views and a bind group per shader pass per frame.
- Trail cursor redraw pressure:
  - frontends/rioterm/src/renderer/mod.rs:792-800 keeps Renderer::needs_redraw true while trail_cursor is animating.

Rollback evaluation
- Rolling back 6702381d48 might feel smoother only if it pushes users back toward game-mode/default workaround behavior or stops event-mode shader animation from being driven correctly. That would hide the cost rather than explain it.
- A rollback risks reintroducing the Rio #1603 class of Wayland/event-mode repaint and resize failures, and makes the upstreaming story weaker because the generic event-renderer fix remains unresolved.
- Keep rollback as a diagnostic toggle only: compare full defaults with YAZELIX_TERMINAL_RENDER_STRATEGY=game, but do not treat that as the product fix unless measurement proves event-mode scheduling itself is the bottleneck with effects disabled.

Proposed way forward
1. Add a reproducible Helix j/k workload or tightly specified manual protocol to tools/yazelix_benchmark.py. Capture YAZELIX_TERMINAL_FRAME_LOG and summarize frame pacing with tools/yazelix_benchmark.py frame-summary.
2. Run a config matrix under comparable launch env:
   - vanilla upstream Rio with clean RIO_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config/rio and LD_LIBRARY_PATH unset;
   - yazelix-terminal event mode with no custom shaders and no trail cursor;
   - yazelix-terminal event mode with WebGPU only;
   - yazelix-terminal event mode with one shader and no trail;
   - yazelix-terminal packaged defaults;
   - packaged defaults plus YAZELIX_TERMINAL_RENDER_STRATEGY=game as diagnostic only.
3. Add an explicit no-effects/baseline escape hatch or profile in the wrapper/package so users can launch yzt without contaminating host Rio config and without shader/trail defaults.
4. If the matrix confirms shader cost, patch GhosttyShaderBrush to reuse per-size source/intermediate textures and avoid rebuilding per-pass bind groups where possible; then reconsider SHADER_ANIMATION_WINDOW and animation coalescing.
5. Only narrow or rollback the event-system fix if no-effects yazelix-terminal still performs worse than vanilla Rio under the same backend/config/env.

Verification already run during this investigation
- python3 tools/yazelix_benchmark.py self-test
- python3 tools/yazelix_benchmark.py frame-run --help
- git show --stat --oneline 6702381d48
- source reads for application.rs, screen.rs, renderer/mod.rs, ghostty_shaders/mod.rs, and misc/yazelix_terminal_config.toml

Implication for yzt-upstream-event-system-fixes-tg9
- Do not propose the event-system fix upstream until the Helix latency bead has at least one clean no-effects comparison. If no-effects yzt matches upstream Rio, the upstream PR can stay focused on #1603/event-mode repaint behavior while shader performance remains a fork-local follow-up.
close_reason: Completed investigation and benchmarking surface: added no-effects baseline profile, helix-jk and helix-viewport benchmark workloads, package smoke coverage, preliminary A/B metrics, and follow-up optimization bead yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0. Event fix rollback remains diagnostic-only; next patch should target viewport repaint latency first.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Implementation/investigation update, 2026-06-01

User clarified the practical Helix latency trigger: it is not always plain j/k movement. It happens often when the cursor reaches Helix's top/bottom scroll-margin area, roughly the top/bottom five viewport lines, and continued movement causes the editor viewport to follow.

Changes made
- Added packaged no-effects baseline config at misc/yazelix_terminal_config_baseline.toml.
- Added YAZELIX_TERMINAL_PROFILE=baseline and YAZELIX_TERMINAL_EFFECTS=none support in misc/yazelix_terminal_desktop.sh. This keeps packaged WebGPU/font/window defaults but removes custom shaders and trail-cursor for A/B tests.
- Updated pkgRio.nix to install share/yazelix-terminal/baseline/config.toml and substitute the baseline config home into the wrapper.
- Hardened tools/yazelix_event_mode_smoke.sh so package-default checks ignore ambient host/Yazelix RIO_CONFIG_HOME and YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG, then verify baseline profile and game-mode composition.
- Extended tools/yazelix_benchmark.py with config templates: wgpu-no-effects, wgpu-shader-event, yzt-default, yzt-default-game.
- Added helix-jk workload for stable cursor churn and helix-viewport workload for Helix-like scroll-margin viewport movement. helix-viewport models the top/bottom five-line margin and repaints visible rows when the viewport follows the cursor.

Local verification
- python3 -m py_compile tools/yazelix_benchmark.py
- python3 tools/yazelix_benchmark.py self-test
- python3 tools/yazelix_benchmark.py frame-run --help
- sh -n misc/yazelix_terminal_desktop.sh tools/yazelix_event_mode_smoke.sh
- nix build .#yazelix-terminal-fast -o result_yazelix_terminal_fast_package
- tools/yazelix_event_mode_smoke.sh ./result_yazelix_terminal_fast_package
- git diff --check

Preliminary debug-binary metrics, stable cursor churn (helix-jk, 4000 moves, target/debug/rio)
- wgpu-no-effects: 90 presented frames, render median 3.33ms, p95 3.93ms, p99 23.16ms, process CPU 28.8%.
- wgpu-shader-event: 83 presented frames, render median 2.49ms, p95 5.73ms, p99 31.25ms, process CPU 28.9%.
- yzt-default event mode: 53 presented frames, render median 6.63ms, p95 10.38ms, p99 78.26ms, process CPU 32.7%.
- yzt-default game mode: 75 presented frames, render median 11.64ms, p95 14.96ms, p99 41.28ms, process CPU 51.8%.

Preliminary debug-binary metrics, viewport-follow churn (helix-viewport, 400 moves, target/debug/rio)
- wgpu-no-effects: 5 presented frames, render median 383.82ms, max 422.37ms, process CPU 67.7%.
- wgpu-shader-event: 4 presented frames, render median 194.60ms, max 431.86ms, process CPU 59.1%.
- yzt-default event mode: 29 presented frames, render median 6.58ms, p95 269.21ms, p99 603.97ms, max 694.25ms, process CPU 73.4%.
- yzt-default game mode: 41 presented frames, render median 11.06ms, p95 212.86ms, max 293.96ms, process CPU 80.3%.

Interpretation
- Stable cursor movement supports the earlier shader/trail suspicion: full yzt defaults add render-duration and CPU overhead, and game mode is not a product fix.
- The user's actual viewport-follow trigger is broader than shader cost. The helix-viewport workload produces large render spikes even with no effects, so the next patch should target viewport row repaint/render batching first. Shader/trail optimization remains useful, but it is not the only cause.
- Do not rollback the event-system fix as the first patch. Keep game mode as a diagnostic only. The preferred path is to instrument and optimize dirty-row/visible-row repaint cost, then re-run the no-effects/full-effects matrix.

Follow-up created
- yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0 tracks the actual optimization work and depends on this investigation bead.

title: Prevent yazelix-terminal from contaminating host Rio state
description:
Problem:
Yazelix Terminal currently has launch paths that can inherit or preserve host/process Rio environment. In particular, the desktop wrapper honors an existing RIO_CONFIG_HOME before selecting the packaged Yazelix config, and the package wrapper currently prefixes LD_LIBRARY_PATH for the Rio process. During vanilla Rio diagnostics, the Yazelix shell exported RIO_CONFIG_HOME=/home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/yzxterm, which contaminated plain `rio` launches and made upstream reproduction harder.

Practical implication today:
Installing or running yazelix-terminal must not make a normal user's existing Rio install read Yazelix-generated config, inherit Yazelix app identity, or receive package-only loader paths. A user should be able to open host Rio before, during, or after Yazelix Terminal work and see host Rio behavior.

Scope:
- Define the environment contract for packaged yazelix-terminal versus plain upstream Rio.
- Make the yazelix-terminal desktop/launcher wrapper ignore unrelated inherited RIO_CONFIG_HOME unless an explicit Yazelix-owned override is supplied, such as YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG.
- Ensure package-only LD_LIBRARY_PATH changes are process-local to yazelix-terminal and do not leak into child shells or nested GUI app launches.
- Keep app identity and config paths Yazelix-owned, reviewable, and distinct from host Rio defaults.
- Add a smoke test or documented manual check that launching `rio` from a Yazelix Terminal session uses host Rio config, not yzxterm config.

Acceptance criteria:
- Plain host `rio` launched from a normal shell is unaffected by installing or using yazelix-terminal.
- Plain host `rio` launched from inside a Yazelix Terminal shell does not inherit YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG-derived RIO_CONFIG_HOME.
- The packaged yazelix-terminal launcher still uses the generated Yazelix config when invoked by Yazelix.
- README or runtime docs state the isolation contract and the allowed env vars.
close_reason: Implemented wrapper and child-process env isolation: desktop wrapper ignores ambient host RIO_CONFIG_HOME, package marks private loader/config env for sanitization, PTY/fork/daemon child launches scrub Rio/Yazelix launch-only env and restore host LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Verified with sh -n, cargo fmt, cargo test -p teletypewriter yazelix_child_command, git diff --check, and nix eval package pname.

title: Investigate recurring Yazelix crash in fork-added redraw/event code
description:
Problem:
A recurring Yazelix runtime crash was observed after the Rio fork work, and the prior Yazelix investigation note points at fork-added yazelix-terminal code rather than vanilla upstream Rio. The next crash investigation should stay fork-first and use local crash/lifetime evidence to prove which added redraw/event path is involved.

Scope:
- Focus on code added in this fork; treat vanilla Rio as a control only after fork-side suspects are exhausted.
- Correlate the crash with terminal lifetime/exit evidence from the crashed launch before the current healthy launch, not with the current launch.
- Inspect event-mode scheduling in frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs.
- Inspect shader/redraw dirty rearming in frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs.
- Inspect cursor trail timing in frontends/rioterm/src/renderer/trail_cursor.rs.
- Inspect Ghostty shader redraw timing in sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs.
- Prefer concrete artifacts: process lifetime logs, crash backtrace/core/journal evidence, redraw scheduling traces, and minimal reproduction commands.

Acceptance criteria:
- The crashed terminal instance is distinguished from the current running/healthy instance with concrete timestamps, PIDs, or lifetime records.
- Every listed fork-added code area is either implicated with evidence or ruled out with a specific reason.
- Any candidate root cause explains why the issue would appear in yazelix-terminal after the fork and not in original Rio.
- A minimal reproduction, diagnostic patch, or next narrower bead is produced if the root cause is not fully fixed.

Notes:
This follows the fork-focused investigation note added to the main Yazelix crash bead: yazelix-investigate-recurring-yazelix-crash-after-runtim-bmdfb.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Comparison evidence, 2026-06-02:

User reports they are currently running Yazelix inside vanilla/upstream Rio rather than the yazelix-terminal fork, and that this setup is much more stable. The focus-regain lag plus rapid catch-up rendering bug does not occur at all in vanilla Rio under the same Yazelix workload.

Interpretation for this bead: keep the investigation fork-first. This is negative evidence against Yazelix app/Zellij/Codex workload as the primary cause and positive evidence for code added in yazelix-terminal after the Rio fork, especially event-mode redraw scheduling, Ghostty shader/trail animation timing, and terminal pending_update/redraw interactions.

description:

Acceptance criteria:

title: Optimize Helix viewport repaint latency
description:
Problem:
The Helix latency report is concentrated around scroll-margin viewport changes: when the cursor enters the top/bottom margin and movement continues, Helix shifts the editor viewport and many visible rows change. The new helix-viewport benchmark shows this path can produce very large render-duration spikes even with no shaders/trail cursor, so rolling back the event-system fix is unlikely to be the right first patch.

Scope:
- Use tools/yazelix_benchmark.py helix-viewport as the synthetic repro, then confirm with real Helix key injection or manual frame logs.
- Add enough renderer instrumentation to separate PTY parsing, visible-row snapshotting, dirty row rebuild count, grid upload, shader passes, and presentation pacing.
- Optimize the viewport repaint path first: damage coalescing, dirty-row snapshot/rebuild cost, grid buffer reuse/upload, and avoiding unnecessary full rebuilds.
- Re-evaluate shader/trail effects after the no-effects viewport path is under control.

Acceptance criteria:
- Repro protocol includes Helix scroll-margin behavior near top/bottom five viewport lines.
- Baseline metrics are recorded for wgpu-no-effects, wgpu-shader-event, yzt-default, and yzt-default-game.
- Patch reduces no-effects helix-viewport render-duration tail and does not regress stable helix-jk.
- Decision on event-system rollback is explicit; rollback should remain diagnostic-only unless no-effects event mode still loses after viewport repaint optimization.
- Verification commands and artifact paths are documented in the bead.
notes:
Implementation update, 2026-06-01

Patch shape
- Added frame-log phase metrics: renderer_run, terminal_lock_wait, terminal_snapshot, snapshot_visible, panel_collect, grid_emit, sugarloaf_render, panel/row rebuild counts, and terminal_lock_busy_count. tools/yazelix_benchmark.py now summarizes those fields.
- Removed terminal locks from render-only color/scrollbar/IME cursor paths by using renderable_content snapshots.
- Removed the pre-render grid-size terminal lock in Screen::render; grids are ensured after Renderer::run snapshots dimensions.
- Renderer::run now uses a bounded 8ms unfair terminal-lock wait for non-full damage. If the terminal is still busy, it leaves pending_update dirty, records terminal_lock_busy_count, and application.rs schedules a vblank-paced retry instead of immediate hot-looping.
- PTY reader now caps each locked parser batch at 16 KiB and backs off for 4ms when terminal damage is already in flight, so sustained Helix-like output gives the renderer chances to snapshot intermediate states instead of racing to the final cursor/viewport state.

Key finding
- The original no-effects viewport spikes were not row snapshot cost: snapshot_visible stayed below ~0.1ms. The pathological tail was terminal-lock contention and parser/render coalescing. Removing incidental render locks fixed the lock wait, but needed explicit PTY backoff to avoid losing paint cadence.

Final debug-binary viewport matrix, target/debug/rio, helix-viewport 400 moves
- wgpu-no-effects: artifacts/benchmarks/frame_time/yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0/no-effects-viewport-final-after-scrollbar-move/summary.json: 72 presented frames, render median 11.85ms, p95 13.76ms, p99 19.13ms, max 31.32ms, terminal_lock_busy total 0.
- wgpu-shader-event: artifacts/benchmarks/frame_time/yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0/final-wgpu-shader-event-viewport/summary.json: 72 presented frames, render median 11.79ms, p95 12.51ms, p99 18.32ms, max 27.81ms.
- yzt-default event: artifacts/benchmarks/frame_time/yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0/final-yzt-default-viewport/summary.json: 155 presented frames, render median 11.95ms, p95 16.88ms, p99 19.20ms, max 27.58ms.
- yzt-default game: artifacts/benchmarks/frame_time/yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0/final-yzt-default-game-viewport/summary.json: 78 presented frames, render median 24.41ms, p95 26.64ms, p99 37.41ms, max 62.70ms.

Stable cursor check
- wgpu-no-effects helix-jk rerun: artifacts/benchmarks/frame_time/yzt-optimize-helix-viewport-latency-yg0/no-effects-helix-jk-4ms-backoff-rerun/summary.json: 46 presented frames, render median 7.38ms, p95 9.66ms, p99 20.98ms, max 29.28ms, terminal_lock_busy total 0.
- This is not a clean win versus the earlier 90-presented-frame preliminary sample, but presented delta stayed reasonable (median 10.83ms, p95 15.32ms). Treat the cursor benchmark as noisy and keep it as a follow-up watchpoint if users still feel cursor-only movement is worse.

Rollback decision
- Do not rollback the event-system fix. The reproduced no-effects viewport pathology was parser/render lock scheduling and damage coalescing, not the resize/event fix itself. Rollback remains diagnostic-only.

Verification
- cargo fmt
- cargo check -p rioterm
- cargo build -p rioterm --bin rio
- uv run python -m py_compile tools/yazelix_benchmark.py
- uv run ruff check tools/yazelix_benchmark.py
- uv run ty check tools/yazelix_benchmark.py
- uv run python tools/yazelix_benchmark.py self-test
- env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH uv run python tools/yazelix_benchmark.py frame-run ... for the listed artifacts
- git diff --check
close_reason: Implemented viewport latency fix: added phase metrics, removed render-only terminal locks, bounded render lock waits, paced retries, capped PTY parser lock batches, and added PTY in-flight backoff. Final no-effects viewport run improved from prior ~383ms median/~422ms max render duration to ~11.85ms median/~13.76ms p95/~31.32ms max with 72 presented frames. Event-system rollback remains diagnostic-only; created follow-up yzt-helix-cursor-cadence-after-viewport-fix-f1n for residual cursor-only cadence validation.

title: Preserve one-line cursor warp trail
description: The cursor trail jump clamp is too rigid after yzt-clamp-cursor-trail-tui-scroll-gfr. It snaps long horizontal warps even when the cursor only moved one row vertically, which removes the good-looking Helix/Yazi behavior where holding j/k animates one-line vertical movement and column changes across short/empty lines. Tune the policy so redraw-scale jumps still snap, but one-row vertical cursor movement may animate even when the column delta is large. Apply the same intent to CPU trail geometry and WGPU Ghostty shader previous-cursor uniforms. Acceptance: one-row vertical long-column warp animates; large multi-row/screen jumps still snap; focused tests cover both paths.
