comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Implemented row-aware cursor trail snap tuning. The previous clamp snapped any jump over 8 cells, which removed desirable Helix/Yazi one-row vertical cursor warps with large column corrections. New policy: one-row vertical movement never snaps solely because of horizontal distance; general redraw-warp snap threshold is raised to 32 cells; multi-row/screen-scale jumps still snap. Applied to both CPU trail cursor geometry and Ghostty shader previous/current cursor uniforms. Validation: cargo fmt -- --check; cargo test -p rioterm trail_cursor; cargo test -p sugarloaf --features "wgpu rio-window/x11 rio-window/wayland rio-window/wayland-dlopen" cursor; cargo check -p rioterm --features wgpu; git diff --check.

title: Reproduce Rio event-mode repaint bug before upstream PR
description:
Problem:
We should not open an upstream Rio PR for the Yazelix event-system fix unless the current upstream bug is reproducible on vanilla Rio. Historical evidence and fork-side behavior are not enough; if current upstream no longer reproduces the event-mode repaint/resize failure, the right action is to close or retarget the upstreaming effort rather than submit a speculative fix.

Scope:
- Reproduce the Rio #1603-style event renderer bug on current vanilla upstream Rio, not yazelix-terminal.
- Use a clean launch environment: RIO_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config/rio and LD_LIBRARY_PATH unset unless the test explicitly records otherwise.
- Capture concrete evidence: command lines, Rio version/commit, renderer config, compositor/session, process under Rio, expected vs actual behavior, and screenshot/video or frame/log artifact where possible.
- Try both event/default renderer and game-mode workaround only as a contrast.
- If no reproduction is possible after a serious attempt, document that upstream PR should not be opened and decide whether the fork fix remains local-only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Current upstream Rio commit/version and launch environment are recorded.
- A deterministic or manually repeatable reproduction protocol is documented.
- Reproduction evidence exists for event-mode failure, or a negative reproduction report explains attempts and why no PR should be opened.
- yzt-upstream-event-system-fixes-tg9 is updated with the result before any upstream PR is drafted.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Viewport-trigger clarification, 2026-06-01

User clarified that the event-system bug only happens when the viewport changes. The upstream repro protocol must therefore resize/change the viewport while the child process is otherwise quiet; plain idle typing, cursor movement, or steady-output tests are not sufficient. In practice the repro should launch vanilla upstream Rio in event/default renderer mode, run a quiet child such as sleep or a paused shell, change the window/terminal viewport size, and capture whether repaint/resize stalls until PTY output arrives. Game mode remains only the workaround contrast.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Related negative evidence, 2026-06-02:

User reports that running Yazelix inside vanilla/upstream Rio is stable and does not exhibit the yazelix-terminal focus-regain lag plus rapid catch-up rendering bug. Do not use that focus-burst symptom as evidence for an upstream Rio bug.

This does not by itself close the separate viewport-change repaint repro requested by this bead, but it narrows the current lag/catch-up symptom toward fork-added yazelix-terminal code rather than vanilla Rio.

title: Fix Yazelix cursor shader parity in yzxterm
description: Investigation showed Yazelix Terminal renders the shader path correctly. The parity bug is in main Yazelix materialization: the generated yzxterm config keeps the packaged default shader chain instead of honoring the active yazelix-cursors sidecar selection/effects. Child terminal rendering work is not needed unless the main-repo fix exposes a renderer issue.

title: Yazelix Terminal window opens but does not receive keyboard input
notes: 2026-05-31 corrected diagnosis: Rio reads RIO_CONFIG_HOME, not RIO_CONFIG. The desktop wrapper now sets RIO_CONFIG_HOME to the packaged config directory by default, preserving user-provided RIO_CONFIG_HOME, and validates YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG as a config directory containing config.toml. The packaged config sets [renderer] strategy="game" to work around the upstream Rio Wayland/COSMIC idle input/resize stall tracked in raphamorim/rio#1603. Manual test: maintainer confirmed the corrected desktop launch accepts typing and resize.

title: Exit when X11 window is destroyed
description: COSMIC/Xwayland close can destroy or unmap the Rio X11 window without delivering the normal WM_DELETE CloseRequested path. Rio currently skips WindowEvent::Destroyed, leaving the process alive with no usable window. Treat Destroyed as a terminal window close path and verify with gtk-launch plus xdotool windowclose.

Verified cargo fmt --check, nix develop -c cargo check -p rioterm, nix develop -c cargo build -p rioterm, python3 tools/yazelix_conformance.py verify, gtk-launch input smoke, shell exit smoke, and xdotool windowclose process-exit smoke.

title: Evaluate upstreaming Yazelix event-system fixes
description:
Evaluate whether the fork's event-system/render wakeup fixes should be proposed upstream to Rio.

Scope:
- Identify the exact fork changes involved in event-mode rendering, Wayland wakeup/presentation behavior, and cursor-shader redraw scheduling.
- Separate generic Rio fixes from Yazelix-specific packaging/config/app-id behavior.
- Check whether the fix is still relevant against current Rio upstream and whether upstream has an equivalent change.
- Reproduce the current upstream event-mode repaint/resize bug before drafting any PR.
- Propose the smallest upstreamable patch set only if current upstream Rio still reproduces the bug.
acceptance_criteria:
- Source paths and commits/patches for the relevant fork changes are listed.
- Generic vs Yazelix-specific pieces are explicitly classified.
- Current upstream Rio status is checked and recorded.
- The Rio #1603-style event-mode repaint/resize bug is reproduced on current vanilla upstream Rio with concrete evidence, or a negative reproduction report explicitly blocks opening an upstream PR.
- Recommended upstream PR shape, tests, and risk notes are documented only after the reproduction gate is satisfied.
notes:
Current findings recorded 2026-06-01:

- Primary upstream target is Rio #1603: event-based renderer stops repainting/resizing until PTY output wakes it.
- Rio #1354 is related scheduling/context evidence; Rio #892 is only social/process evidence, not the technical target.
- Public upstream behavior looks agent-friendly enough for a narrow PR: no anti-AI policy found, #1609 disclosed LLM help and was effectively upstreamed via maintainer PR #1611, and multiple Claude Code PRs remain in queue.
- Current local repro uses vanilla upstream Rio outside Home Manager, installed from /home/lucca/pjs/open_source/yazelix_related/rio with nix profile install path:/home/lucca/pjs/open_source/yazelix_related/rio#rio. Verified command -v rio => /home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/rio and rio --version => rioterm 0.4.6. The failed Home Manager pkgs.rio install and failed Cargo install are recorded in comments.
- Launch diagnostics: current Yazelix shell exports RIO_CONFIG_HOME to the Yazelix terminal config and LD_LIBRARY_PATH to Nix store libs, so vanilla checks should use env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH RIO_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config/rio. Under that clean override, rio -e sleep 30 stayed alive until timeout. The nix-profile desktop entry used relative Exec=rio/TryExec=rio and may fail in desktop launchers; a local ~/.local/share/applications/rio.desktop override with absolute Exec/TryExec was added and validated.
- Recommended first PR shape: generic Rio fix for #1603, minimal diff, clear reproduction, before/after screenshot/video, source paths, test commands, and plain agent-assistance disclosure.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Investigation note, 2026-06-01

Upstream target
- Primary target: Rio #1603, "Terminal window stops responding to resize requests if a process is running under Rio without output". This matches the event-based renderer failing to repaint/respond unless PTY output wakes it. The reported workaround is renderer.strategy = "game", but users report extra CPU/power cost.
- Secondary context: Rio #1354, "renderer.target-fps is not working". This is in the same event-rendering/scheduling area and points at the earlier event renderer rewrite, but it should be supporting evidence rather than the first PR target.
- Not the target: Rio #892. That issue is useful social/process evidence for AI-assisted event fixes, but technically it is about Quit action dispatch being dropped.

Upstream maintainer/agent-friendliness evidence
- No CONTRIBUTING.md, PR template, or anti-AI contribution policy was found in raphamorim/rio. Contribution requirements are implicit.
- PR #1609 explicitly disclosed LLM assistance. The maintainer responded politely, said he would test locally, asked the contributor to test his follow-up branch, and then merged PR #1611 with changes originally implemented in #1609. This is a strong positive signal for useful AI-assisted work, even if the maintainer may prefer to rework it.
- Several recent open PRs disclose Claude Code generation, including #1614 for #892. They were not auto-closed. This suggests agent-generated PRs are tolerated, but not that large AI PRs will merge quickly.

Local repro setup
- Installed vanilla nixpkgs Rio normally through Home Manager as pkgs.rio, not through a RIO_CONFIG_HOME wrapper. Verified: rio --version prints rioterm 0.4.5.
- Plain rio uses the default config path, ~/.config/rio/config.toml. Current config is intentionally minimal: confirm-before-quit = false.
- Added grim and slurp for Wayland screenshots. Useful commands: grim ~/Pictures/rio-repro.png, or grim -g "$(slurp)" ~/Pictures/rio-repro-selected.png.
- Earlier custom wrappers rio-vanilla-events/rio-vanilla-game were removed because they changed the config path and were less representative of a normal user install.

Recommended upstream PR shape
- First PR should target #1603 narrowly: event renderer should repaint/resize without waiting for terminal output.
- Frame it as a generic Rio bugfix, not a Yazelix feature. Avoid Yazelix app-id, launcher, config, packaging, and host-mode changes.
- Include concrete reproduction, before/after screenshots or video, exact source paths, and test commands.
- Keep agent disclosure plain: prepared with agent assistance, manually reviewed and tested.
- Expect maintainer review/rework; small patches are more likely to land than a large event-system bundle.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Repro setup correction, 2026-06-01

The initial Home Manager pkgs.rio install did not open for the user and was removed from Home Manager. A Cargo install of rioterm 0.4.5 was attempted next, but the resulting ~/.cargo/bin/rio failed immediately due glibc/loader mixing from the Nix Rust toolchain. That Cargo install was removed.

Current active vanilla Rio install is outside Home Manager via the upstream Rio flake from /home/lucca/pjs/open_source/yazelix_related/rio, installed into the user Nix profile with:

  nix profile install path:/home/lucca/pjs/open_source/yazelix_related/rio#rio

Verified command resolution and version:

  command -v rio => /home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/rio
  rio --version => rioterm 0.4.6

The separate upstream checkout was updated to origin/main at 7e18dde and sparse checkout was disabled because the flake package needs the full workspace crates. Home Manager no longer carries pkgs.rio.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Rio launch/desktop-entry diagnostics, 2026-06-01

Observed while trying to open the current vanilla upstream Rio install:

- The shell used by the agent/user is inside Yazelix and exports RIO_CONFIG_HOME=/home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/yzxterm. Running plain `rio` from that environment is therefore not a vanilla Rio config-path test; it loads the Yazelix terminal config unless RIO_CONFIG_HOME is overridden.
- The shell environment also had LD_LIBRARY_PATH set to Nix store libraries. Some non-Nix X11 tools failed under that environment due glibc/loader mixing. Clean launch tests should use `env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH ...`.
- With `env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH RIO_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config/rio RIO_LOG_LEVEL=INFO timeout 20s rio --enable-log-file -e sleep 30`, Rio initialized successfully, created a Vulkan device/swapchain, spawned `sleep`, and stayed alive until `timeout` killed it at 20s. This suggests the current upstream-flake binary is not immediately crashing when launched with an explicit vanilla config path.
- User observed normal window launch closes after a couple seconds. That still needs a clean no-`-e` launch check from outside Yazelix env, but the `sleep` test proves the window/event loop can stay alive.
- User also observed the desktop entry does not open at all. The installed nix-profile desktop entry at ~/.nix-profile/share/applications/rio.desktop uses relative `TryExec=rio` and `Exec=rio`. Desktop launchers often do not include ~/.nix-profile/bin in PATH, so this can silently fail.
- Added a user-local override at ~/.local/share/applications/rio.desktop with absolute `TryExec=/home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/rio` and `Exec=/usr/bin/env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH RIO_CONFIG_HOME=/home/lucca/.config/rio /home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/rio`. Validated with desktop-file-validate.

Implication for upstream repro: always record launch environment. For vanilla Rio bug evidence, use RIO_CONFIG_HOME=$HOME/.config/rio and avoid inheriting Yazelix-specific config/env from the current terminal.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Helix latency cross-link, 2026-06-01

Created yzt-helix-input-latency-vs-rio-5xy to investigate the user-observed Helix j/k responsiveness gap between vanilla upstream Rio and yazelix-terminal. Initial code read suggests this should block upstream PR confidence until a clean no-effects comparison is done. The likely fork-local confounder is packaged WebGPU + three custom shaders + trail-cursor, with Ghostty shader state re-arming a 650 ms redraw window on cursor movement. Keep the #1603 upstream PR shape narrow, but wait to confirm that no-effects yzt does not regress against vanilla Rio before proposing the event-system fix upstream.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
PR gate correction, 2026-06-01

Do not open an upstream Rio PR unless the event-mode repaint/resize bug is reproduced against current vanilla upstream Rio first. If the current upstream no longer reproduces the issue, the upstream PR should not be opened; instead document the negative reproduction and decide whether the fork fix remains local-only or needs retargeting. Added blocker bead yzt-reproduce-rio-event-bug-1dk and made this upstreaming bead depend on it.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Viewport-trigger clarification, 2026-06-01

Do not treat non-viewport checks as a valid upstream repro. The target failure requires a viewport/window-size change while event/default renderer is waiting and the child process is not producing output. If current upstream Rio repaints/resizes correctly under that condition, the event-system PR should not be opened.

title: Desktop launch does not present focused Wayland window
description: Clicking the Yazelix Terminal desktop entry can start Rio without presenting a usable window on COSMIC/Wayland. The launcher provides XDG_ACTIVATION_TOKEN/DESKTOP_STARTUP_ID, but Rio removed those variables during environment setup before RouteWindow could read and apply them to the first window.
close_reason: Fixed by preserving DESKTOP_STARTUP_ID/XDG_ACTIVATION_TOKEN until RouteWindow applies the startup activation token, then relying on existing router cleanup before child spawn. Verified with targeted Nix-toolchain test, package rebuild, and visible COSMIC/Wayland desktop launch screenshot.
